senses.definition: a larger and more populated town

examples.x: She enjoys exploring the city's parks and landmarks on weekends.
They are planning a city tour to discover hidden gems and local attractions.
They visit the city's museums to learn about its history and culture.
This city is known for its diverse population and multicultural atmosphere.
We often take weekend trips to nearby cities for sightseeing and relaxation.

senses.definition: an area with human population that is smaller than a city and larger than a village

examples.x: She likes to walk around town and visit the local shops.
She volunteers at the town library to help with organizing books.
They decorate the town square for holidays and special occasions.
They organize community events in town to bring people together.

senses.definition: a public path for vehicles in a village, town, or city, usually with buildings, houses, etc. on its sides

examples.x: I crossed the street carefully at the pedestrian crosswalk.
The street was filled with colorful houses and blooming flowers.
They walked down the busy street, lined with shops and restaurants.

senses.definition: a financial institution that keeps and lends money and provides other financial services

examples.x: Can you recommend a reliable bank for opening a new account?
I went to the bank to deposit some money into my savings account.
She checked her account balance using the bank's mobile app.
They visited the bank to apply for a loan to purchase a car.
We used the ATM outside the bank to withdraw money quickly.

senses.definition: a large building where sick or injured people receive medical treatment and care

examples.x: I visited my friend at the hospital and brought her some flowers.
She went to the hospital for a check-up with her doctor.
They work as nurses at the hospital, taking care of patients' needs.
We donate blood regularly at the hospital to help those in need.
We saw a newborn baby in the maternity ward of the hospital.

senses.definition: a place where we pay to sit and eat a meal

examples.x: He works as a chef in a popular restaurant.
She left a positive review online for the restaurant where she ordered a pizza.
They celebrated their anniversary at a fancy restaurant overlooking the city.
They have a children's menu at the restaurant, making it family-friendly.
We ordered takeout from our favorite restaurant and enjoyed it at home.

senses.definition: a place where we go to watch movies

examples.x: He likes the comfortable seats and big screen at the movie theater.
I enjoy watching the latest movies at the movie theater with my friends.
She bought popcorn and soda at the movie theater before the film started.
They are planning a movie night at the movie theater with their family.
We visit the movie theater occasionally to escape into a different world through films.

senses.definition: a large store that we can go to and buy food, drinks and other things from

examples.x: He works as a cashier at the supermarket, scanning and bagging customers' items.
I buy groceries and household items at the supermarket every week.
My father compares prices at different supermarkets to get the best deals.
She prefers shopping at the supermarket because of the wide variety of products available.
We use reusable bags when shopping at the supermarket to reduce plastic waste.

senses.definition: a place where we can send letters, packages, etc., or buy stamps

examples.x: I like to check my mailbox at the post office every day for any new mail.
I went to the post office to mail a package to my friend.
She bought some stamps at the post office to send out her letters.
They offer various postal services, such as express delivery and registered mail, at the post office.
They visited the post office to pick up a registered letter.

senses.definition: a long and hard seat that is normally made of metal or wood and two or multiple people can sit on

examples.x: I like to sit on the bench and watch people walking by.
She found a comfortable bench to rest and read her book.
She tied her shoelaces on the bench before going for a run.
They gathered around the bench to have a group discussion.

senses.definition: a place where important cultural, artistic, historical, or scientific objects are kept and shown to the public

examples.x: He marveled at the dinosaur skeletons in the natural history museum.
I enjoyed the temporary exhibition at the museum, which featured contemporary art from around the world.
I visited the museum to learn about ancient civilizations.
She admired the artwork displayed in the museum.
She was inspired by the paintings and sculptures created by renowned artists in the museum.

senses.definition: a large public place in a town or a city that has grass and trees and people go to for walking, playing, and relaxing

examples.x: He flew a kite in the park on a sunny day.
I enjoy having picnics in the park with my family.
The children were happily playing in the park.
They had a friendly soccer match with their friends in the park.
We sat on a bench in the park and watched people playing sports.

senses.definition: a building where we give money to stay and eat food in when we are traveling

examples.x: Can you recommend a budget-friendly hotel in the city center?
I forgot my toothbrush, so I asked the hotel staff for a replacement.
I stayed at a luxurious hotel during my vacation.
She booked a room at a beachfront hotel for her summer getaway.
They checked out of the hotel and headed to the airport for their flight.

senses.definition: (plural) a group of people whose job is to catch thieves, killers, etc. and make sure everyone follows rules

examples.x: She felt safer knowing that the police were patrolling the neighborhood.
She thanked the police for their quick response in handling the emergency situation.
The police apprehended the thief and recovered the stolen items.
They admire the police for their commitment to maintaining law and order.
They rely on the police to investigate crimes and bring criminals to justice.

senses.definition: an image that shows where things like countries, seas, cities, roads, etc. are in an area

examples.x: He studied the map to plan the best route for his road trip.
I used a map to navigate my way through the city.
She discovered a hidden gem by exploring the less-known areas on the map.
She unfolded the map to find the location of the tourist attractions.
We followed the map's directions to reach the hiking trail.
